structured.title: Rachel Plans Funding And Holiday Logistics
structured.overview: Rachel and the user discuss several practical family and church-related topics while getting ready to leave. They talk about where to go for the outing, childcare and bathroom organization at home, and plans for a summer project to repaint a corridor. A major part of the conversation focuses on church funding and Rachel’s potential move to full-time work: they work through salary figures, tax, student fees, and how much extra the church would need per month to support her full time. The user suggests using advanced resources for sabbatical planning and training, and Rachel agrees there is useful material to review. The conversation ends with family travel and holiday logistics, including the children, flights, and getting everyone into the car.

action_items.description: Create advanced resources playlist in correct order

apps_results.content:
## Travel timing and route
- Leaving soon; unsure exact timing
- No bay booking available
- Plan: drive around, maybe IKEA/Asda area

## Caffeine and health
- Rachel stopped Red Bull since Wednesday
- Says caffeine addiction is gone
- E. coli helped her quit

## Home and church plans
- Bathroom needs tidying and wall strips
- Summer project: paint corridor white
- Best done when kids are away

## Full-time funding discussion
- Need full-time support for two years
- Net pay: 1135; gross: 1208
- Total need estimated around 39k

## Church funding options
- Church should cover part of salary
- Extra monthly support may be needed
- Could use two-year or three-year commitment

## Training, resources, and next steps
- Advanced website has useful sabbatical resources
- Ryan, Sarah, Ben, and Ellen need training
- Next: review resources and build a playlist
